alpha_s.definition: Dimensionless coupling of QCD controlling quark-gluon interaction strength; it runs with scale due to asymptotic freedom.

alpha_em.definition: Dimensionless QED coupling that sets the strength of electromagnetic interactions.

g_fermi.definition: Effective weak coupling in four-fermion theory; appears in low-energy charged-current processes.

m_z.definition: Pole mass of the neutral weak gauge boson.

m_w.definition: Pole mass of the charged weak gauge boson.

m_h.definition: Pole mass of the Higgs scalar associated with electroweak symmetry breaking.

m_top.definition: Pole mass of the top quark, the heaviest Standard Model fermion.

gamma_w.definition: Total decay width of W±; inverse of lifetime in natural units.

gamma_z.definition: Total decay width of Z⁰, constraining invisible neutrino channels.

sin2_theta_w.definition: Electroweak mixing parameter controlling W/Z composition from SU(2)L and U(1)Y gauge fields.

ckm_matrix.definition: Unitary 3×3 matrix relating quark weak eigenstates to mass eigenstates.
ckm_matrix.formula: V_{\text{CKM}} = \begin{pmatrix} V_{ud} & V_{us} & V_{ub} \\ V_{cd} & V_{cs} & V_{cb} \\ V_{td} & V_{ts} & V_{tb} \end{pmatrix}

cross_section.definition: Probability measure for a scattering process, obtained from amplitude-squared integrated over phase space.

mandelstam_s.definition: Invariant mass squared of the initial state; equal to centre-of-mass energy squared.

mandelstam_t.definition: Invariant momentum transfer between initial and final legs in 2→2 kinematics.

phase_space.definition: Kinematically allowed final-state momentum manifold with Lorentz-invariant measure dΦn.

feynman_diagram.definition: Graphical representation of a perturbative amplitude contribution in quantum field theory.

su3_color.definition: Non-Abelian QCD gauge symmetry under which quarks are triplets and gluons are octets.

relic_density.definition: Present-day dark-matter energy density fraction, typically quoted as Ωh².

freeze_out.definition: Epoch when annihilation rate falls below Hubble expansion and comoving DM abundance decouples.

branching_ratio.definition: Fraction of decays proceeding through a given channel, BR_i = Γ_i/Γ_total.

decay_table.definition: Structured list of kinematically allowed decay channels with partial widths and branching ratios.

parent_particle.definition: Unstable state whose decay channels are evaluated in the decay calculation workflow.

parameter_scan.definition: Systematic sweep of model parameters to study observable response, constraints, and preferred regions.

scan_target.definition: Named model quantity or observable chosen as the objective of a parameter sweep.

scan_scale.definition: Linear or logarithmic spacing policy used to distribute parameter grid points.

mc_events.definition: Number of generated phase-space samples used to estimate integrals and statistical uncertainties.
